groups.context_description: Term names divine giving as the expression of covenant faithfulness — God's giving of land, promise, and blessing as the embodiment of his inner covenantal commitment
Term names divine giving as the expression of inner love and compassion — God gives in direct response to inner need, longing, prayer, or suffering
Term names the giving of inner-being capacities — wisdom, spirit, understanding, songs, and strength given directly to the inner life
Term names divine giving as moral judgement — God gives into the hand of enemies or destruction as the moral expression of his inner response to faithlessness
Term names the inner act of human giving — self-giving, sacrificial giving, votive giving, or giving as the expression of inner orientation toward God or community
Term names the giving or withholding of glory and honour — the inner-being act of ascribing worth to God, or divine withholding of glory from others
Term names eschatological or restorative giving — God gives to transform inner conditions: ashes to beauty, mourning to gladness, faint spirit to praise

Term declares God's inner being as good — the foundational doxological assertion that God's character, name, steadfast love, and Spirit are good, and that his goodness governs his relationship to all he has made
Term names human moral character and conduct as good — the inner quality of the person who walks uprightly, acts honestly, and whose character is recognised and assessed as good before God and others

Term names goodness as inner experiential good — what is genuinely good for the human person: proximity to God, worship, waiting, communal harmony, and the reorientation of the inner life toward what truly satisfies

Term names comparative wisdom good — the better-than sayings of wisdom literature where inner and relational qualities are ranked as the greater good above material wealth, social status, or external abundance
Term names God's good word and promise — the covenantal faithfulness through which God declares, fulfils, and sustains his promised good toward his people

Term names the moral assessment of conduct, ways, or deeds as not good — the prophetic and wisdom verdict that certain inner orientations and behavioural expressions are contrary to the good God requires
Term names goodness as an inner-being disposition and Spirit-produced fruit — a quality that fills the person and is completed by God, expressed in righteous conduct and resolve
Term names kindness as God's inner disposition of generous goodwill toward humanity — the divine attribute that leads to repentance, governs judgment and mercy, and is displayed supremely in Christ
Term names kindness as a Spirit-produced inner quality of the believer — the disposition to act with goodness toward others, listed as fruit of the Spirit and garment of the renewed person
Term names the righteous person's inner moral character — the constitutive orientation of the whole inner being toward what is right before God and others
Term names righteousness as the standard by which God tests and vindicates — the inner moral condition God sees, approves, rewards, and which may be abandoned
Term names the inner response of the righteous person toward God — orientation of trust, joy, and praise
Term names righteousness as the inner moral character of the person — right relationship with God and others expressed in just conduct, faithful dealing, and integrity
Term names righteousness as the status credited to or vindicated in the person by God — moral standing before God, including imputation
Term names righteousness as the divine character quality received, longed for, and responded to — gift, stream of salvation, ground of inner hope and exultation
Term names the inner-being quality of innocence — the condition of the heart and hands being free from moral guilt before God
Term characterizes the person as morally innocent or free from guilt — the inner moral standing of a person before God and the human community
Term identifies "innocent blood" as a moral category — the shedding of which constitutes moral pollution and the protection of which is a moral obligation before God
Term marks freedom from moral obligation or liability — clearance from a sworn commitment, legal claim, or culpability
Term characterizes the person as morally innocent or free from guilt — the inner moral standing of a person before God and the human community
Term identifies "innocent blood" as a moral category — the shedding of which constitutes moral pollution and the protection of which is a moral obligation before God
Term marks freedom from moral obligation or liability — clearance from a sworn commitment, legal claim, or culpability
Term names what covers the person in death and judgment — the inverted image of pride and pomp brought to humiliation before God
Term frames the self-presentation of a person before God or another — offering the self in availability, surrender, or accountability
Term frames a first-person or communal confessional disclosure of inner condition — guilt, fear, inadequacy, need, or grief — before God
Term frames a divine disclosure of God's inner orientation — election, delight, care, and relational approach toward his people
Term frames a moral verdict or exposure of the inner being — what God sees, seeks, or judges in the heart, will, and conduct of persons
Term frames a personal disclosure of the inner experience of God — searching, hiddenness, helplessness, or submission before divine power
Term frames a disclosure of inner knowing, wisdom, awe, or affective orientation — including fear of the Lord, understanding, love, grief, and longing
Term marks the temporal extent or ongoing duration of an inner-being state, divine relationship, or orientation — establishing how long the inner condition has persisted or been sustained
Term names the place associated with the moral abomination of child sacrifice — an act explicitly contrary to the divine inner will and a marker of the depths of human spiritual corruption
Term characterizes the person as inwardly blameless and whole before God — the quality of heart and conduct that constitutes right standing and walking in God's presence
Term characterizes the person as inwardly blameless and morally whole — the quality of inner character that defines one who fears God and turns from evil
Term characterizes the beloved as wholly complete and perfect — the wholeness that evokes delight and singular devotion
Term names the absence of soundness as the condition of the person or people under divine discipline or moral corruption — linking physical and inner brokenness
Term names the Thummim as the instrument of divine disclosure of inner moral guilt — the means by which God reveals what is hidden in the person or community
Term names the inner-being quality of innocence — the condition of the heart and hands being free from moral guilt before God
Term names a metaphorical covering of moral reproach — the public declaration of innocence and vindication
Term names the absence of covering as the condition of inner vulnerability and exposure — of the poor, and of the realm of death before God
Term frames covering as divine response to vulnerability and divine expression of grief — God's compassion for the exposed and his inner mourning made visible
Term frames the self-presentation of a person before God in obedience, availability, or acknowledgment of inadequacy
Term frames a first-person or communal disclosure of inner need, grief, fear, or moral condition before God
Term introduces a theophanic vision or divine encounter — making the divine presence directly present to perception
Term introduces a divine disclosure of God's orientation of love, promise, care, or attentiveness toward his people
Term introduces a moral verdict, exposure of inner sin or deception, or inner transformation through divine action
Term introduces inner wisdom reflection, longing, moral restraint, or the recognition of inner truth
Term introduces the inner experience of anguish, dread, despair, hope, or emotional intensity before God or in life
Term names integrity as the inner moral condition of the person — the wholeness and blamelessness of heart and conduct that characterizes the one who walks rightly before God
Term names integrity as the inner quality that the person holds fast and presents before God — the moral wholeness that defines character and guides conduct under pressure
Term characterises God's way, works, and knowledge as perfect — the inner perfection of divine character and action as the ground of trust
Term characterises the Torah as perfect — its completeness as the source of inner renewal of the soul
Term names the eschatological transformation of the person — the putting on of incorruptibility and immortality at resurrection
Term names incorruptibility as the quality of inner love and the inner orientation of those who seek God's glory
Term names moral and relational purity as an inner character disposition — the quality of heart and motivation governing conduct and relationships
Term describes the purity of the heart and inner person as moral and spiritual integrity before God — an inner life free from corruption and oriented toward God
Term describes purity attributed to God's word and the fear of the Lord — the quality of inner spiritual integrity that characterises divine revelation and right orientation toward God
Term describes the covenantal and spiritual restoration of the inner person — being made clean before God through divine action, removing inner defilement and renewing covenantal relationship
Term describes ritual/levitical cleanness as a covenantal condition governing access to God and sacred space — the state of being clean as prerequisite for worship and community life
Term names purity as an inner character quality among the dispositions through which authentic apostolic ministry is expressed
Term describes the morally pure inner character — the quality of heart, mind, and motivation that is free from defilement and moral corruption
Term describes relational and covenantal purity — inner disposition of fidelity and undivided orientation in relationship to Christ or within human relationships
Term names the act of inner moral and spiritual purification — soul, heart, and self purified through obedience, hope, and turning toward God
Term names ritual purification as an act of preparation expressing inner orientation of readiness and covenantal reverence toward God
Term names the structured purification process as an act of ritual preparation expressing inner readiness and covenantal participation
Term qualifies inner motivation — names pure and sincere motivation as the inner disposition from which authentic action ought to flow, in contrast to selfish ambition
Term describes the purification of the inner person from sin and moral defilement — the heart and soul made pure through divine cleansing, repentance, and spiritual renewal
Term describes ritual cleansing as a covenantal act of transition — the person made ritually clean through prescribed acts, enabling restored participation in sacred community
Term describes God's purifying action upon priests, Levites, and people — cleansing for restored covenantal service and communal life
Term names the prescribed purification procedure as the covenantal pathway governing a person's return to community and sacred participation
Term names purification as a covenantal service and inner-oriented commitment — the person setting their heart toward God even where external cleanness requirements are imperfectly met
Term names the ritual period of purification following childbirth — the covenantal state of the post-partum woman and her transition back to full community participation
Term describes the absolute moral purity of God's character — an inner quality of divine nature that is incompatible with evil or wrongdoing
Term names the ritual purification period as a state of cleansing — the post-partum woman's covenantal condition during and after the prescribed period of purification
Term names righteous acts and decrees as expressions of divine righteousness or inner moral character — where the standard or act directly engages covenantal standing and moral orientation
Term names sincerity as an inner quality of unmixed, genuine motivation — the condition of acting from pure inner integrity before God rather than from calculation or performance
Term names inner purity or sincerity — the quality of being unmixed, genuine, and morally uncontaminated in inner disposition
Truth as transforming/shaping force on the inner person — sanctification, purification, self-orientation
Truth as the proper object of the will — accepting, obeying, refusing, or rejecting truth as inner act
Truth as inner quality of character — integrity, authenticity, living and speaking truthfully
Truth as the Spirit's identity and work in the inner person — Spirit of truth
Truth as relational/theological identity — who Christ is; worship in spirit and truth
Truth as the gospel received inwardly — knowledge of truth as saving and formational event
Genuineness as inner-being orientation — true worship, turning to the true God, inner fidelity
Truthfulness as inner character quality — integrity, genuineness, the true heart
"Truly" as qualifier of inner-being recognition, knowing, confession, and reception
Truthful speech as inner act of integrity — in relationship and spiritual growth
Uprightness as an inner quality of the heart — tested by God, contrasted with wickedness, invoked as protection
Uprightness as the path/way — inner orientation expressed in the direction of life
Uprightness expressed in speech — words as disclosure of inner integrity
Uprightness as the standard and quality of right judgment — divine and demanded of human authorities
Uprightness as inner character formation — wisdom's goal; the inmost being rejoices at rightness
Uprightness resisted, absent, or refused — the inner being's failure to receive or produce what is right
Uprightness of heart directed toward God — inner moral orientation of relational fidelity
Regnal moral assessment — "did right/what was right in the eyes of the Lord" — moral orientation of the will under divine evaluation
"Right in his own eyes" — moral self-determination; inner conscience as its own arbiter
"The upright" / "upright in heart" — uprightness as stable inner moral character, especially in Psalms and Proverbs
Uprightness as divine character — God and his word described as upright/right
Job as the upright man — blameless and upright as defining inner character under testing
"God made man upright, but they have sought out many schemes" — original moral constitution and its departure
Straightforwardness as honest inner character — rightness of speech, claims, and conduct
Uprightness as the defining moral character of Christ's kingship — inner quality expressed in sovereign rule
"Before the face/presence of the Lord" — relational orientation of prayer, lament, and honest speech toward God
"Before their faces" — the stumbling block of iniquity as the primary object of inner attention (Ezekiel)
"Directly forward" — inner directedness; focused and undistracted attention
